item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it until I remembered that the ancient Egyptians thought hearts were for thinking, then I loved it :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A very high heart rate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Most of the previous commenters think it is a failed superweapon. I disagree. I think it's a superweapon looking for the right person to hold it! (like the myth of Excalibur stuck in the stone until a righteous knight pulls it out)

Imagine this weapon in the hands of someone who can handle an insanely high heart rate. What traits would it give that someone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's actually just Latin for "relic" (at least according to google translate). It's a historical artifact, and 'reliquia' sounded cool, so in it went.

E: Internet says that the word 'relic' does technically have a religious connotation. Huh. I'm still running with it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No the author, but "reliqua" appears to be derived from "reliquary", which is a shrine for keeping sacred relics, specifically relics of saints. I'm assuming that this is because the SCP has some kind of religious significance, as it's tied to the Ancient Egyptian belief that the heart, not the brain, was the place where the soul/thought came from. It was also found in the tomb of Wegaf/Ugaf, who was Pharaoh, and since Pharaohs were thought to be descended from Ra, it's considered a religious artifact, hence the term "reliqua".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Explain the reason behind the object class.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wasn't always a failure - it's just been a while since it's last maintenance… Maybe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No vote. Not sure what I didn't like about this— I like your play on the heart idea and the fact that it isn't a very good super-weapon. Maybe the imagery reads a bit too cartoony.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But… we took an ancient sword and refinished it?

o_O

Holy crimes against antiquity Batman!

:'(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: holy crap when did you get back

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I almost downvoted, until I realized that it's a fucked up superweapon attempt. All the upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Failed super weapon attempt. Like it a lot. Surprised there aren't more "failed" SCPs. I think it's completely different than the katana aside from being a weapon that is worse than useless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A weapon what actively discourages Fightan Good. I likes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it a lot. It reminds me of the Mellified Man in which it's about ancient peoples using their arcane knowledge to do something our feeble science cannot fathom, and then screwing it up because they didn't know something a six-year-old would know today.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's the point. See my opening post for an explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounds about right. Fixed, and thanks.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I kinda don't get it. It seems to do precisely the opposite of what it was intended to.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure "refurbished" is the right word to use, here. "Restored" seems to fit better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(12:03:42 AM) Scantron: Dexanote: what does "Ha HA" mean?
(12:04:09 AM) Dexa: didn't know how to articulate "AWWWW SHIEEEET YOU MADE A LIVING SWORD."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A sword pacemaker. I like it. Nice imagery, well-written, cool effect, & from a culture I adore. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ha HA.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I always thought it was cute how the Egyptians thought the heart was the seat of thought, emotions, etc. I just sort of combined that with the common idea in fiction that they had alchemical/magical knowledge we didn't, along with my desire to make a sword SCP that doesn't suck. Well, I mean, it sucks, but that's in-universe.

I'm not expecting it to be exceedingly popular, but I'm confident that it's a good piece.

E: I'm aware that this SCP is similar to the Katana of Apparent Invincibility. I'm hoping that they go in different enough directions with it for people to appreciate the former.

E: And I'm going on vacation tomorrow (I should be in bed right now to wake up in time to leave). Interestingly enough, this isn't the first time I've left the morning after posting an article. Anyways, I'll answer critique and make changes a few days from now when I get the chance.
